extra_model_fields.follow_up_action_for_verifier: After submission, open the CSVs and recompute one packet (use the Verification sheet) and confirm totals match Final_Decision_Table and memo claims.
extra_model_fields.priority_rationale: Medium - changes are incremental but important for verifiability; unlikely to require redesign of decision logic.

extra_model_fields.follow_up_action_for_verifier: After submission, open the CSVs and recompute one packet (use the Verification sheet) and confirm totals match Final_Decision_Table and memo claims.
extra_model_fields.priority_rationale: Medium - changes are incremental but important for verifiability; unlikely to require redesign of decision logic.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: CSV files present in artifacts/ (decision_logic.csv, final_decision_table.csv) and include formula_text and computed_value columns for every non-empty cell; verifier can open them with standard tools.
planned_changes.description: Add CSV/plain-text exports for Decision_Logic and Final_Decision_Table containing: sheet_name, row_id (packet id), column headers, computed_value for each cell, and a separate column with the cell formula text where applicable.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Final_Decision_Table shows per-pack cost columns with formulas that reference Inputs! constants; total estimated cost is non-negative and computed via formula for each row.
planned_changes.description: Add explicit per-pack cost columns to Final_Decision_Table: Rework_Cost, Retest_Cost, Reject_Cost, Total_Estimated_Cost. Implement formula references to Inputs! cost constants and Decision_Logic flags (Leak_OK, Balance_OK, Critical_Fail).

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Sensitivity sheet contains at least 5 perturbation scenarios per metric and a summary table with counts and aggregated costs; formulas reference Final_Decision_Table outputs.
planned_changes.description: Expand Sensitivity sheet with a wider grid of threshold perturbations and add aggregated outcome rows showing pass/warn/fail counts and scenario total cost (sum of Total_Estimated_Cost) per scenario.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Verification sheet contains clear stepwise calculations and cell references that a human verifier can follow to recompute the example and obtain the same numeric result.
planned_changes.description: Add a Verification sheet with a worked example for one packet (preferably P2) showing input cells, intermediate Decision_Logic cells (with cell refs), and the final cost computation step-by-step.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: formulas_export.txt exists and maps each important logical cell to its formula text and a concise explanation.
planned_changes.description: Export a plain-text file (formulas_export.txt) listing key formulas with sheet and cell coordinates for Decision_Logic and Final_Decision_Table to aid parsers lacking openpyxl.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Memo updated in [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] and references the new files and Verification sheet; checklist instructs a verifier how to recompute one packet.
planned_changes.description: Update quality_gate_memo.md to: (a) include exact sheet/cell references for representative formulas; (b) document deterministic assumptions about rework modeling; (c) add a short 'How to validate' checklist describing the CSV/text exports and the worked example.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Workbook contains no macros and uses standard formulas only; a quick manual check confirms openable in common spreadsheet software.
planned_changes.description: Confirm workbook compatibility: remove any nonstandard features, avoid macros, and ensure formulas use standard Excel functions compatible with openpyxl/xlsxwriter.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Close reproducibility gap (xlsx parse limitation) and clarify per-pack cost outputs, sensitivity aggregation, and explicit verification steps so human verifiers and automated graders can validate formulas and numeric outputs without third-party dependencies.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline submission produced both required artifacts and a clear memo describing decision logic, representative formulas, and a final decision table. The model grader assigned semantic score ~0.95 and the verifier accepted the result with a single primary warning: the .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so cell-level verification was not performed. Provide small, machine-readable exports and explicit per-pack cost outputs to remove reproducibility gaps and to make the workbook independently verifiable.
